artifact.content_preview:
From: dave.chen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: carol.nguyen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration
Date: March 7, 2026

Alice, Carol,

While reviewing the network migration plan for Site A, I wanted to flag
an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

Given that our contractors will already be on-site and the network
infrastructure will be partially disrupted during the Catalyst 9500
switch deployme

extracted_tasks.text: Discuss Wi-Fi infrastructure upgrade opportunity with the steering committee
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I wanted to flag an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

extracted_tasks.text: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points to Catalyst 9120 series across Sites A, B, and C
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Upgrading to the Catalyst 9120 series during the same migration window would avoid a separate disruptive maintenance activity

extracted_tasks.text: Obtain steering committee approval for the expanded scope and budget
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Could we add this to the scope and get steering committee approval before the March 18 window?

extracted_tasks.text: Decision: Use the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window for concurrent infrastructure upgrades
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: this would be an ideal time to also upgrade the Wi-Fi access points across all three data centre facilities.

metadata.subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration

alerts.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of on-site work.
alerts.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: 'Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites' — Wi-Fi access point hardware is distinct from the in-scope switching infrastructure and is not referenced anywhere in the scope baseline. CONSTRAINTS: 'Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclus
alerts.suggested_action: Do not proceed. The project manager must formally reject this request as out-of-scope and log it in the project change register. If the business case is compelling (EOL hardware, cost avoidance), Dave Chen should be directed to submit a formal Change Request (CR) for steering committee review prior

    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",

    "D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due December 15, 2024",

    "D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due March 10, 2026",
    "D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due April 30, 2026",
    "D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due May 31, 2026",
    "D-008: Project Closure Report — Due June 30, 2026"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes",
    "Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure",
    "Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board",
    "Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)",
    "Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval",
    "All work must comply with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach",
    "All work must comply with the Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "All work must comply with the Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "All work must comply with the Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "All client-facing deliverables must comply with the Official Languages Act",
    "Scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process",

  ]

in_scope: Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure
Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A
Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A
Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A
Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites
Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones
BGP routing table migration and validation
Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A
AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4
Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4
End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments
Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration
Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative
deliverables: D-001: Project Management Plan — Due October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due December 15, 2024
D-003: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package — Due January 31, 2025
D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due February 28, 2025
D-005: User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan — Due March 10, 2026
D-006: Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) — Due April 30, 2026
D-007: Post-Migration Validation Report — Due May 31, 2026
D-008: Project Closure Report — Due June 30, 2026
constraints: Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes
Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure
Budget variances exceeding 15% require submission to Treasury Board
Go-Live (D-006) must be achieved no later than April 30, 2026
Final project closure date is June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)
Extensions beyond June 30, 2026 require ADM-level approval
All work must comply with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management — A Lifecycle Approach
All work must comply with the Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy
All work must comply with the Treasury Board Policy on Government Security
All work must comply with the Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)
All client-facing deliverables must comply with the Official Languages Act
Scope, schedule, or budget changes must follow the SSC Project Change Management Process
Scope changes require written approval from the Project Sponsor and must be assessed for cost and schedule impact before implementation

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.15,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of on-site work.",
  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: 'Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites' — Wi-Fi access point hardware is distinct from the in-scope switching infrastructure and is not referenced anywhere in the scope baseline. CONSTRAINTS: 'Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes' and 'Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure.'",
  "suggested_action": "Do not proceed. The project manager must formally reject this request as out-of-scope and log it in the project change register. If the business case is compelling (EOL hardware, cost avoidance), Dave Chen should be directed to submit a formal Change Request (CR) for steering committee review prior to the March 18 window. The CR must include: (1) a scope change justification, (2) a revised budget estimate triggering a Director General-level budget amendment given the $85K–$110K addition, (3) a schedule impact assessment against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint, and (4) confirmation that the additional work remains compliant with ITSG-33 and Treasury Board Policy on Government Security. The project manager should also caution that informal vendor discussions (TechSupplier contact) must cease until a CR is formally approved, to avoid unauthorized commitment of public funds.",
  "reasoning": "The request to upgrade wireless access points is a clear scope expansion. The approved scope covers Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switch deployment and network segmentation/BGP work — wired switching infrastructure only. Wi-Fi access point replacement is a separate hardware category not mentioned anywhere in the in-scope items, out-of-scope exclusions, or deliverables, meaning it was never evaluated or budgeted. The estimated $85,000–$110,000 incremental cost represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase against the $2,400,000 ceiling, which while below the 15% Treasury Board variance threshold, still requires a formal Director General-approved budget amendment under the stated constraints. The 9-day schedule addition also carries risk to the April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline given the document is dated March 7, 2026 — less than eight weeks before Go-Live. The logical efficiency argument (contractors already on-site, network disruption already occurring) has operational merit and may justify a CR, but it does not constitute authorization. The informal vendor engagement described by Dave Chen is also a procurement governance concern that should be flagged separately."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of on-site work.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: 'Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites' — Wi-Fi access point hardware is distinct from the in-scope switching infrastructure and is not referenced anywhere in the scope baseline. CONSTRAINTS: 'Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes' and 'Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure.'
parsed_json.suggested_action: Do not proceed. The project manager must formally reject this request as out-of-scope and log it in the project change register. If the business case is compelling (EOL hardware, cost avoidance), Dave Chen should be directed to submit a formal Change Request (CR) for steering committee review prior to the March 18 window. The CR must include: (1) a scope change justification, (2) a revised budget estimate triggering a Director General-level budget amendment given the $85K–$110K addition, (3) a schedule impact assessment against the April 30, 2026 Go-Live constraint, and (4) confirmation that the additional work remains compliant with ITSG-33 and Treasury Board Policy on Government Security. The project manager should also caution that informal vendor discussions (TechSupplier contact) must cease until a CR is formally approved, to avoid unauthorized commitment of public funds.
parsed_json.reasoning: The request to upgrade wireless access points is a clear scope expansion. The approved scope covers Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switch deployment and network segmentation/BGP work — wired switching infrastructure only. Wi-Fi access point replacement is a separate hardware category not mentioned anywhere in the in-scope items, out-of-scope exclusions, or deliverables, meaning it was never evaluated or budgeted. The estimated $85,000–$110,000 incremental cost represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase against the $2,400,000 ceiling, which while below the 15% Treasury Board variance threshold, still requires a formal Director General-approved budget amendment under the stated constraints. The 9-day schedule addition also carries risk to the April 30, 2026 Go-Live deadline given the document is dated March 7, 2026 — less than eight weeks before Go-Live. The logical efficiency argument (contractors already on-site, network disruption already occurring) has operational merit and may justify a CR, but it does not constitute authorization. The informal vendor engagement described by Dave Chen is also a procurement governance concern that should be flagged separately.
